description_long: This object is a vintage-style CD player with a rectangular, boxy form and a hinged lid that opens upward, revealing the disc compartment and control panel. The exterior is primarily matte black, with metallic and plastic accents visible on the front and inside. The front panel features several tactile control buttons, indicator lights, and a display screen, as well as a disc tray and a tonearm, suggesting it may also function as a turntable. The interior includes a circular platform for the disc, surrounded by various switches and dials, and the lid appears to be solid and protective. The overall build is robust, with visible hinges and mechanical components on the sides. This device is designed to rest on flat surfaces and is suitable for use in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or community centers.
description: A matte black, boxy CD player with a hinged lid, front control panel, and visible disc tray, featuring metallic and plastic details.
description_view_0: The left side of the CD player is visible, showing the open lid and part of the disc compartment, with some internal components exposed.
description_view_1: The rear or side view highlights the hinges and mechanical parts, with the lid partially open and minimal detail on the visible panel.
description_view_2: The right side reveals the open lid, disc compartment, and some control elements, with a clearer view of the internal structure.
description_view_3: The front view displays the control panel, disc tray, buttons, indicator lights, and the open lid, providing the most interactive features.
